subject: Streetlight out on Bohland Ave near 19th
body_clean: Mayor Okonkwo, the streetlight at the corner of Bohland and 19th Avenue has been dark for over three weeks. I walk my dog there every night and it is genuinely unsafe — that stretch is pitch black and the bushes on the corner make it worse. I've mentioned it to a neighbor who said she already called the village once. Can someone please get it repaired before someone gets hurt?

subject: Streetlight out on Bohland Ave near 19th
body_clean: Ms. Wojcik, thank you for writing. We have submitted a repair order to ComEd for the light at Bohland and 19th Avenue and flagged it as a safety priority. ComEd typically responds within 7–10 business days, but we will follow up if we don't see action by next week. We appreciate your patience.

subject: Speeding on Eastern Ave — someone is going to get killed
body_clean: Mayor, I live on Eastern between 25th and 26th and the speeding has gotten completely out of control this summer. I sat on my porch Sunday evening and counted at least eight cars in one hour doing what looked like 50-plus mph. My kids play in front of this house. We pay our taxes. I want to know what is being done and I want an actual answer, not a form letter.

subject: Speeding on Eastern Ave — someone is going to get killed
body_clean: Mr. Tillman, I hear you. I've added Eastern Avenue between 25th and 26th to our directed patrol rotation for the next 30 days — you should see marked units there during peak evening hours, typically 5 to 9 p.m. I'll also talk to the traffic sergeant about requesting a speed trailer. If you observe specific plates or incidents, please call the non-emergency line at 708-555-0142 and ask for me directly.

subject: Dangerous intersection at Washington and 50th Ave
body_clean: Good afternoon Mayor Okonkwo. I want to bring to your attention the intersection of Washington Blvd and 50th Avenue. There is no stop sign on the 50th Ave approach and drivers simply do not yield. I personally witnessed a near-miss last Tuesday morning around 8:15 involving a minivan and a cyclist. With school traffic in that area this feels like an accident waiting to happen. Is a stop sign or traffic study possible?

subject: Dangerous intersection at Washington and 50th Ave
body_clean: Ms. Nduka, thanks for the detail. We'll put Washington and 50th on the list for a traffic count study — I can have a crew out there with counters within two weeks. If the data supports it, adding a stop sign on the 50th approach is a straightforward fix. Washington Blvd is a Village road so we have full authority to act. I'll send you findings when the study wraps up.

subject: Neighbor parking on lawn — 1402 Marshall Ave
body_clean: I have been calling the village for two months about the property at 1402 Marshall. The man there parks two trucks on his front lawn, sometimes three vehicles, right on the grass. The lawn is now just dirt and gravel and it looks terrible. I was told someone would come out and inspect. Nobody ever came. I'm a homeowner, I care about this street, and I would appreciate someone actually following through this time.

subject: Neighbor parking on lawn — 1402 Marshall Ave
body_clean: Ms. Ewing, I sincerely apologize for the gap in follow-through. I pulled the file — a prior inspection notice was sent in June but no follow-up visit was ever scheduled, which is an error on our end. I personally inspected 1402 Marshall this afternoon. Vehicle storage on the lawn violates Bellwood Municipal Code Section 8-4-12. A written notice of violation with a 15-day compliance window was posted today. I will reinspect on August 28th.

subject: Village never responded to my November pothole complaint
body_clean: Mayor, I emailed the village in November about a pothole at Granville and 25th Ave that was swallowing tires. I received an automated acknowledgment and nothing else. The pothole was not filled, got worse through the freeze, and in January I blew out a tire on it. I should not have to come back a second time for something this basic. I want to know what happened to my original request.

subject: Village never responded to my November pothole complaint
body_clean: Mr. Webb, I owe you an apology. Your November request was logged but fell off the repair schedule when we shifted crews to snow operations in December — not an excuse, just an explanation. The hole at Granville and 25th is on tomorrow's cold-patch crew list. Once temperatures stabilize we'll do a permanent repair. If you want to pursue reimbursement for the tire, Village Hall can walk you through the claims process.

subject: Offensive graffiti on PACE bus shelter at Washington and Mannheim
body_clean: Good morning. The PACE bus shelter at Washington Blvd and Mannheim Road has been tagged with graffiti including some racially offensive language. I take the bus there and so do many seniors from my building. It is embarrassing and unwelcoming. I understand this might be PACE property but can the village help move this along? It has been there at least ten days.

subject: Offensive graffiti on PACE bus shelter at Washington and Mannheim
body_clean: Ms. Szymanski, thank you for bringing this to me directly. You're correct the shelter belongs to PACE, but our office has a standing relationship with their operations team and I personally called their facilities line this morning. They confirmed a crew will be out within 48 hours given the nature of the content. I share your concern — that language should not greet anyone waiting for a bus in Bellwood.

subject: Crosswalk at Monroe and 25th barely visible
body_clean: Mayor Okonkwo, the painted crosswalk at Monroe Street and 25th Avenue has faded so badly you can barely see it — especially when the street is wet. I've nearly been hit twice while using it. Drivers clearly don't realize it's there. This seems like a straightforward repainting job. Is there a schedule for crosswalk maintenance in Bellwood, or do these only get done when someone complains?

subject: Crosswalk at Monroe and 25th barely visible
body_clean: Ms. Hayward, that's a fair question and honestly a fair criticism. We do have a crosswalk inspection cycle but it's tied to the annual street maintenance plan and some locations slip through. Monroe and 25th will be on the re-striping list for this fall before the pavement gets too cold. I've also asked Frank DiMeo to look at giving pedestrian crossings more priority in next year's infrastructure review.

subject: Graffiti on retaining wall, Geneva Ave underpass
body_clean: Mayor, there is extensive spray-paint graffiti on both sides of the retaining wall at the Geneva Avenue railroad underpass. It has been there since before Christmas and it is quite vulgar — not something kids should be walking past on their way to the Metra station. I understand weather may slow things down but it has been three weeks. Who handles this, the Village or the railroad?

subject: Graffiti on retaining wall, Geneva Ave underpass
body_clean: Mr. Volkov, the retaining wall at the Geneva underpass is railroad property, but the Village has a standing agreement with Metra to report nuisance conditions on their right-of-way on residents' behalf. We submitted a graffiti abatement request this morning. These typically take 3–4 weeks through the railroad's process, but we'll follow up to keep it moving.

subject: Graffiti on Bellwood Ave utility box still there after 5 weeks
body_clean: I reported graffiti on the electrical utility box at Bellwood Ave and Harvard Ave back in early November. I got a reply saying it would be addressed. It is now mid-December and the graffiti is still there, including some gang-related symbols that honestly make me uneasy. I'd like to know what happened to my original request and when this will actually be cleaned up.

subject: Drag racing on Rice Ave late at night
body_clean: Good morning. I'm on Rice Ave near Granville and the drag racing situation on summer nights has become unbearable. Last Friday it went from about 11 p.m. until nearly 2 a.m. I called 911 once but by the time a car came through they were gone. My toddler woke up twice. Is there any enforcement plan? It has been every weekend this month.

subject: Streetlight strobing on Hirsch Ave near Madison
body_clean: Hi Mayor, the light pole on Hirsch Ave just north of Madison St has been flickering on and off for about two weeks — like a strobe at night. It keeps me up with the blinds open and I worry it could affect someone with photosensitive epilepsy. I believe it is a ComEd pole. Can the village reach out on our behalf?

subject: Three blocks of Englewood Ave completely dark for months
body_clean: Mayor, there are no working streetlights for approximately three blocks on Englewood Avenue between 19th and 22nd. This has been the case since at least December. I am 72 years old and walk to the pharmacy on St. Charles twice a week — this route is simply not safe at night. I have called the village twice. Each time I was told it was reported. It has not been fixed.

subject: Neighbor's fence encroaches on my property — village won't act
body_clean: Mayor Okonkwo, I'm writing because I've exhausted my options with my neighbor at 717 Frederick Ave. He installed a wood fence in the spring that my surveyor says encroaches 14 inches onto my property at 715 Frederick. I've spoken with him twice and he refuses to move it. Code enforcement told me this is a civil matter. I understand that, but is there any village process at all? It feels like the village just doesn't want to get involved.

subject: Signal timing at Mannheim and St. Charles is creating hazards
body_clean: Mayor Okonkwo, I travel through Mannheim Road and St. Charles twice a day and the signal timing there is completely wrong. Left-turn backups on St. Charles block the crosswalk and I've seen pedestrians nearly hit. I know Mannheim is a state road but surely the village has some ability to request a signal retiming from IDOT? This intersection feels more dangerous every month.

subject: Semi trucks using Morris Ave as a cut-through
body_clean: Dear Mayor Okonkwo, large semis have been using Morris Avenue as a cut-through from Mannheim to avoid the St. Charles signal. This is a residential street. The trucks shake the houses and there are kids on this block constantly. Last Tuesday a driver nearly took out my mailbox. Is there a weight limit on Morris Ave and if so is anyone enforcing it?

subject: My noise complaint was not properly investigated
body_clean: Mayor, I filed a noise complaint three weeks ago about a property on Eastern Ave. I was told someone would follow up. What I got was a form letter saying no violation was found. No one knocked on my door. No one told me what was reviewed or when. The noise has continued every weekend. I want to understand what investigation actually took place, because from where I sit nothing happened.

subject: Near-miss at 50th and St. Charles — this intersection needs help
body_clean: Mayor Okonkwo, I witnessed a very serious near-miss yesterday at 50th Avenue and St. Charles Road around 7:45 in the morning. A car ran what I believe was a four-way stop and nearly hit a school bus. The stop signs there are simply not visible enough given the speed of traffic on St. Charles. I think this intersection needs a signal or at minimum flashing red lights. This almost happened right in front of me.

subject: Abandoned car blocking alley behind Harvard Ave
body_clean: Hello, there is a silver Honda Civic that has been sitting in the alley behind the 1200 block of Harvard Avenue for at least three weeks. It has two flat tires and no plates. It is blocking access for garbage trucks — my bin has been skipped twice because of it. I don't know who owns it but it needs to be towed.
